content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES CO14’IMISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660
Fax (916) 322-0886
May 14, 1998
Ms. Cynthia Humbert Neely
City Attorney
City of Stockton
425 North El Dorado Street
Stockton, California 95202
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-98-133
Dear Ms. Neely:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of councilmember Ann
Johnston regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
May Councilmember Johnston contribute current campaign funds to the City of Stockton
to purchase a graffiti trailer for use in the city’s war on graffiti?
CONCLUSION
Yes, under sections 89510-89518, governing the use of campaign funds, Councilmember
Johnston may contribute campaign funds to the City of Stockton to purchase a graffiti trailer
because this use is reasonably related to a governmental purpose and will not have a material
financial effect on the councilmember.
Government Code sections 81000- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-98-133
Page 2
FACTS
Ann Johnston is a councilmember in the City of Stockton. You are requesting advice to
ascertain whether a councilmember may contribute current campaign funds to the city in order to
apply the money to the purchase of a graffiti trailer for use in the city’s war against graffiti. The
graffiti trailer is a vehicle specially equipped to mix paint and eradicate graffiti. As we discussed
by phone, it is expected that numerous individuals, civic groups and local businesses will
contribute toward the purchase of the graffiti trailer. The vehicle will be used by city employees
and volunteers to respond to requests of citizens who report instances of graffiti throughout the
city. The city employees and volunteers then “match up” the paint on the blighted surface and
paint out the graffiti. Councilmember Johnston would not personally use the graffiti trailer.
ANALYSIS
Sections 89510-89518 of the Act set forth the permissible uses of campaign funds. In
general, expenditures of campaign funds to seek office must be reasonably related to a political
purpose, and expenditures associated with holding office must be reasonably related to a
legislative or governmental purpose. Expenditures that confer a substantial personal benefit on
an official must be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section
89512.)
With respect to the use of campaign funds for donations and loans, section 89515
provides as follows:
“Campaign funds may be used to make donations or loans to bona fide
charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit
organizations, where no substantial part ofthe proceeds will have a material
financial effect on the candidate, elected officer, campaign treasurer, or any
individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign
funds held by a committee, or member of his or her immediate family, and where
the donation or loan bears a reasonable relation to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.”
Section 89515, regarding the use of campaign funds for donations to charitable or nonprofit
organizations applies here, instead of section 89516, concerning the use of campaign funds to
purchase or lease a vehicle, because the donation of funds will be made to the City of Stockton
for use in its graffiti removal program, and the graffiti trailer purchased by the City of Stockton
for graffiti removal will not be used by Councilmember Johnston or her campaign.
A similar question was raised in the Engelhardt Advice Letter, No. A-94-267, where we
advised that under section 89515, a city council candidate could contribute campaign funds to the
City of La Habra Heights toward its graffiti removal program.


File No. A-98-l33
Page 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Hyla P. Wagner
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

CONCLUSION: Yes, under sections 89510-89518, governing the use of campaign funds, Councilmember
